title: Brian Puaca
text:
Faculty directory observation
Name: Brian Puaca
Snapshot: 2026-07-21

confidence.rationale: Proposed from an explicit normalized Knowledge Object field.
Proposed from an explicit normalized Knowledge Object field.
Proposed from an explicit normalized Knowledge Object field.

biography: Brian M. Puaca is an historian whose research focuses on modern German history. His book, Learning Democracy: Education Reform in West Germany, 1945-1965, received the New Scholar's Award from the American Educational Research Association (Division F). He has published articles and chapters on school reform, textbooks, democratization, and memory. He is currently working on a new project exploring youth culture, censorship, and comic book burnings in 1950s West Germany. He teaches courses on German, French, and Cold War history as well as European film. Additionally, he teaches an interdisciplinary course on the history of comic books and American society. He is the creator of the award-winning Comic Book Burnings Project (CBBP), a digital humanities site that maps and documents comic book burnings in America after World War II. The CBBP can be found at this address: https://arcg.is/PW1yu0
education_entries: Ph D in History, University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
MA in Modern European History, University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
BA in History and Germanic Studies, Indiana University
research_interests: Modern Germany; History of Education; Democratization; Memory; Comics
teaching_interests: 20th Century Germany; Modern France; European Film; Postwar Europe
publications: Journal Article, Academic Journal (2024). "The Desire for Peace is 'Unconstitutional' in Bonn": The Free German Youth and Rearmament in West Germany, 1945-1952 . German Studies Review. Volume, 47. Issue, 2. Pages, 253-272.
Book, Chapter in Scholarly Book-New (2023). "Memory and Commemoration of War" in the Oxford Handbook on World War II . Oxford University Press. Pages, 600-616.
Book, Chapter in Scholarly Book-New (2017). The Pen is Mightier Than the Sword?: Student Newspapers and Democracy in Postwar West Germany . Berghahn Books. Pages, 137-157.
Book, Chapter in Scholarly Book-New (2015). Mastering the Past? Nazism and the Holocaust in West German History Textbooks of the 1960s . Springer International Publishing. Pages, 357-373.
Journal Article, Academic Journal (2011). Teaching Trauma: The Second World War in West German History Textbooks . New German Critique; Duke University Press. Issue, 112. Pages, 135-153.
Book, Chapter in Scholarly Book-New (2009). ‘The Appeal to the German Mind’: Educational Reconstruction in the American Zone of Occupation, 1944-1949 . Palgrave. Pages, 105-128.
Monograph (2009). Learning Democracy: Education Reform in West Germany, 1945-1965 . Berghahn Books. Pages, 221.

Journal Article, Academic Journal (2008). Navigating the Waves of Change: Political Education and Democratic School Reform in Postwar West Berlin . History of Education Quarterly. Volume, 48. Issue, 2. Pages, 244-264.

Journal Article, Academic Journal (2005). ‘We learned what democracy really meant’: The Berlin Student Parliament and School Reform in the 1950s . History of Education Quarterly. Volume, 45. Issue, 4. Pages, 615-624.
awards_honors: Allen Ellis Digital Research Award, Popular Culture Association. (2026)
Class of 2013 Faculty Development Award, CNU. (2018)
New Scholar's Book Award, American Educational Research Association. (2011)
DAAD Cornell University Summer Seminar, Deutscher Akademischer Austauschdienst (DAAD). (2008)
Fulbright German Studies Seminar, Fulbright Commission. (2007)
Claude A. Eggertsen Prize, History of Education Society. (2006)
